projectCategories.label: Print-based
projectCategories.description: Projects primarily delivered as physical printed matter.

projectCategories.label: Digital
projectCategories.description: Projects primarily delivered through screens, devices, networks, or electronic files.

projectCategories.label: Hybrid
projectCategories.description: Projects combining physical and digital components.

projectTypes.label: App
projectTypes.description: A software application or interactive digital tool.

projectTypes.label: PDF Report
projectTypes.description: A downloadable electronic document or report.

projectTypes.label: Packaging
projectTypes.description: Packaging for a product or service.

projectTypes.label: Exhibit
projectTypes.description: An exhibit or installation with physical and/or digital components.

projectTypes.label: Campaign
projectTypes.description: A campaign spanning multiple media touchpoints.

projectTypes.label: Social Media Asset
projectTypes.description: A social media post, story, or campaign asset.

criteria.label: Environmental Impact Reduction
criteria.description: The project strategy includes at least one action to reduce environmental impact.
criteria.whyItMatters: A baseline environmental action should be considered across all project types.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to all projects.

criteria.label: Product Ingredients List
criteria.description: The product or product packaging includes a list of components and/or ingredients.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is mainly relevant to packaging or products with material disclosure needs.

criteria.label: Ink, Printing, and Finishes
criteria.description: The project materials were designed to reduce or eliminate the use of inks, varnishes and finishes or require no printing.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is typically not relevant for screen-only outputs.

criteria.label: Paper
criteria.description: The project materials were designed to reduce paper.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion generally does not apply to purely digital projects.

criteria.label: Product Quantity
criteria.description: No more units than needed were produced, including the possibility of no physical copies at all.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies where physical production volume is relevant.

criteria.label: Extended Life Product
criteria.description: When desirable, the product or system is designed to be reused, recharged, repaired and/or upgraded to create an environmental benefit.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is most relevant where a product, system, or durable output can remain in use over time.

criteria.label: Durability
criteria.description: The product or system is physically and highly durable.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion mainly applies to durable physical outputs.

criteria.label: Toxins
criteria.description: The project reduces or eliminates the use of toxic materials.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies primarily when physical materials are selected.

criteria.label: Supplier Environmental Policy
criteria.description: The majority of suppliers have a publicly published environmental policy.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant when suppliers or vendors are part of the project.

criteria.label: Designing Entity Environmental Policy
criteria.description: The designing entity has a publicly published environmental policy.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to most projects.

criteria.label: Material Usage and Waste Reporting
criteria.description: The designing entity has published a quantified report of reductions in material usage and waste disposal.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is more relevant when physical material use is part of the work.

criteria.label: Lifecycle Assessment
criteria.description: The project strategy is supported by a simplified life cycle study or quantitative life cycle assessment.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion may apply to projects with sufficient scale or complexity.

criteria.label: Zero Waste
criteria.description: The final product is a zero-waste product.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion mainly applies to physical products or installations.

criteria.label: Recyclability or Reusability
criteria.description: The design solutions include materials or systems that increase the ease and likelihood of recycling and/or reuse.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies mainly to physical products and materials.

criteria.label: Product End of Life
criteria.description: The product is locally compostable by the end user or can enter a local energy-recovery system that does not involve landfill or thermal treatment.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion mainly applies to physical outputs with material end-of-life considerations.

criteria.label: Renewable Energy
criteria.description: The production of the product uses renewable energy from solar, wind, thermal or tidal sources.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion may apply to printers, hosts, platforms, venues, and other suppliers.

criteria.label: Non-Renewable Resources
criteria.description: The project reduces or eliminates the use of scarce, non-renewable natural resources and raw materials.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion mainly applies to material-based projects.

criteria.label: CO2 and Energy Reporting
criteria.description: The designing entity has published a quantified report of reductions in CO2 emissions and/or energy.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant at the organizational level.

criteria.label: Consumer Energy
criteria.description: The product or system used by the consumer is energy efficient or uses renewable energy.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when the end-user experience consumes electricity or device resources.

criteria.label: Transport
criteria.description: The project strategy includes shipping and/or warehousing efficiencies to reduce transportation and waste.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when physical distribution or warehousing is part of the project.

criteria.label: CO2 Certification
criteria.description: The product displays a recognized certification or third-party declaration of its CO2 emissions.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is mainly relevant when product-level emissions claims are being communicated.

criteria.label: Air Pollution and Greenhouse Gases
criteria.description: Air pollution or greenhouse gas emissions were avoided or reduced.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion can apply across many project types.

criteria.label: Carbon Offsets
criteria.description: The project offsets CO2 emissions for travel, production, and web presence.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when offsetting is part of the strategy.

criteria.label: Water
criteria.description: Water pollution or water use were avoided or reduced.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion mainly applies where materials or production processes use water.

criteria.label: Animal Welfare
criteria.description: The success of the project does not and will not put animals or an entire animal species at risk.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when the project affects ecosystems, sourcing, or wildlife-related outcomes.

criteria.label: Harmless
criteria.description: The project is purposely designed to reduce mental and physical injury to those who produce or use the product.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to all projects.

criteria.label: Health and Safety
criteria.description: The project's main focus is to improve the health or safety of the target audience.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is most relevant when health or safety outcomes are central to the project brief.

criteria.label: Local Labour
criteria.description: Design and production significantly increased the amount of locally sourced labour from the end user's location.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion can apply across project types when labour sourcing is a strategic choice.

criteria.label: Human Rights
criteria.description: The project does not contribute to the violation of universal human rights.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to all projects.

criteria.label: Corruption
criteria.description: In fulfilling their role in the project, no organization was involved in corruption.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to all projects.

criteria.label: Rights of Workers
criteria.description: The project does not contribute to the violation of the fundamental principles and rights of workers.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to all projects.

criteria.label: Electronic Documents
criteria.description: All electronic documents comply with WCAG or PDF/UA accessibility standards.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies to digital or hybrid outputs with electronic documents or interfaces.

criteria.label: Documents and Compliance
criteria.description: All documents comply with WCAG Level AA or PDF/UA accessibility standards.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies to digital or hybrid documents and content systems.

criteria.label: Universal Design Principles
criteria.description: The non-document components of the project adhere to universal design principles.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant across physical and digital experiences.

criteria.label: Intellectual Property
criteria.description: The process or product shares reusable intellectual property that may be valuable to society as a whole.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when reuse, open licensing, or public benefit knowledge-sharing are part of the project.

criteria.label: Affordable
criteria.description: The product is affordable to a wide audience including those with limited financial resources.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when pricing or access barriers are relevant to project outcomes.

criteria.label: Fair Trade
criteria.description: The project involves fair trade.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when labour, sourcing, or geographic equity are central to the project.

criteria.label: Social Gaps
criteria.description: The project's main focus is to encourage closing social gaps of gender, power, or wealth.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is most relevant when equity outcomes are explicit goals of the project.

criteria.label: Freedom of Expression
criteria.description: The project's main focus is to encourage or educate about freedom of expression and/or democracy.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is most relevant when civic participation or expression is central to the project.

criteria.label: Design Education
criteria.description: The project included design students and/or enriches existing design school curriculum.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when educational engagement is part of the project.

criteria.label: Educational Component
criteria.description: The product includes an educational component that promotes sustainability to the consumer.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion can apply across many project types.

criteria.label: Cultural Sustainability Policy
criteria.description: The project or designing entity defines a cultural sustainability policy.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant but may be documented at the organizational level.

criteria.label: Endangered Languages
criteria.description: The project is likely to help preserve endangered languages, including Indigenous languages.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is most relevant when language preservation is part of the brief or audience context.

criteria.label: Indigenous Culture
criteria.description: The project preserves or nurtures Indigenous culture other than language.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is most relevant when Indigenous cultural contexts are involved.

criteria.label: Local Culture
criteria.description: The project's products or services adapt to local and regional cultures.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to most audience-centered communication projects.

criteria.label: Cultural Diversity
criteria.description: The project intends to promote or enhance cultural diversity.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion can apply across many project types.

criteria.label: Supplier Cultural Sustainability Policy
criteria.description: Most suppliers specific to the project have a publicly visible policy of cultural sustainability.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is more relevant when external suppliers materially shape the cultural impact of the project.

criteria.label: Audience Participation
criteria.description: The project includes participation by direct audience members or end users throughout the design process.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant across communication design practice.

criteria.label: Community Participation
criteria.description: The designing entity actively supports and participates in the sustainable development of their community.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant at the organizational and project levels.

criteria.label: Practitioners’ Education
criteria.description: The project results in the education of practitioners regarding sustainable design.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when professional learning or sector influence is part of the outcome.

criteria.label: Economic Benefits
criteria.description: The project strategy identifies and creates economic benefits outside the client and the designing entity.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion can apply across many project types.

criteria.label: Profitability
criteria.description: The project budget or contract does not assume financial loss by the designing entity.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to most projects.

criteria.label: Unpaid Work
criteria.description: There was no process in the project where designers, including student designers, were asked to provide unpaid work.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to all projects.

criteria.label: SMART Goals
criteria.description: A strategy document exists that includes measurable financial objectives.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to projects with defined financial goals.

criteria.label: Accountability and Transparency
criteria.description: The designing entity adheres to accountable and transparent processes and procedures.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion is broadly relevant to all projects.

criteria.label: SOP Improvement
criteria.description: The project resulted in improvements to the designing entity's documented standard procedures.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion applies when internal learning and process improvement are part of project value.

criteria.label: Design Thinking
criteria.description: Design thinking is adding financial value to the audience, users, and/or stakeholders.

applicability.notApplicableMessage: This criterion can apply across many project types.
